task_instructions: ### Objective    This task evaluates how easy it is for prospective postgraduate students to create an account, which is a required step before starting an application.    It explores whether participants can clearly understand what is required, access and interpret key information such as terms and conditions, and navigate the account creation process in an accessible and intuitive way.    ### Note    You will be interacting with a realistic version of the UQ account creation experience. It mirrors the real enrolment process, but it is a safe test environment created for research purposes.    Please use made up details when completing the form. Do not use your real name, email address or personal information.    This task finishes once you reach the screen that allows you to search for programs. The focus is only on account creation.    ### Real-World User Scenario    You have decided to enrol in a Master of Business at UQ. To begin the application process, you must first create an account.    Record your screen and talk through your experience after clicking the link below. You will need to commence this task by **Registering an Account**    * • Share your first impressions of the page. task_instructions: ### Objective    This task evaluates how easy it is for logged-in users to locate security settings within their profile and enrol in Multi-Factor Authentication. It explores clarity, usability, accessibility and user understanding of MFA within the enrolment portal.    ### Real-World User Scenario    You recently heard that one of the online services you use experienced a data breach. Cyber Security is now top of mind for you. As a result, you have decided to strengthen your security across every digital service you use.     You are now logged into your UQ student account and want to enable Multi-Factor Authentication to better protect your information.    Record your screen and talk through your experience.    ### Before you begin:    * In your own words, explain what Multi-Factor Authentication means to you.  * What is your preferred type of MFA, if any? For example, an authenticator app, SMS, email, push notification or something else.   * If you use an authenticator app, which one and why do you prefer it?    ### Then:    * Try to find where you would enrol in MFA from the logged-in student application dashboard. Is it easy to locate security or profile settings?  * Attempt to enrol in MFA. Can you do it successfully?  * Is the process clear and easy to follow? Explain any confusion, friction or accessibility barriers you experience.  * Explain what works well or suggest how the experience could be improved and explain why those changes would help.
